channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady, Signals Only One Cut in 2026 Amid Inflation
item.description: The Federal Reserve kept its benchmark interest rate steady at 3.5%-3.75% for the second meeting in a row. Policymakers cited ongoing inflation concerns and geopolitical risks, like the Iran conflict's impact on energy prices. While the economy is solid and job gains are good, inflation remains high. This led the Fed to signal fewer rate cuts, with projections now showing just one cut in 2026. Stock markets fell, and bond yields rose as investors adjusted to the possibility of higher rates for longer.

item.title: Indian Stocks Recover Amid Geopolitical Jitters and Rupee Woes
item.description: Indian stock markets have bounced back for a third straight session, with the Nifty ending at 23,777 and Sensex at 76,704. This upward move was boosted by investors buying back shares and a shift into sectors like technology. However, ongoing global tensions, oil prices near $109 a barrel, and a falling rupee are dampening enthusiasm. Experts are advising caution, pointing to key resistance levels and expected market swings.

item.title: India Hosiery Prices Jump 7% on Soaring Costs, Demand Uncertain
item.description: The South India Hosiery Manufacturers Association (SIHMA) will implement a 7% price increase across branded innerwear, nightwear, and kidswear, effective Wednesday. This adjustment is driven by a 15-40% surge in raw material costs and a roughly 15% rise in overall production expenses. The move comes as small manufacturers face financial strain from suppliers demanding immediate payments, leading to a 25% production drop, even as broader consumer spending outlook presents mixed signals.

item.title: Oil Spike Hits India Textiles, Squeezing Profits
item.description: India's textile industry faces a severe cost crunch. Raw material expenses, especially for petroleum-based synthetics and dyes, have surged up to 25% as crude oil prices climb past $100 a barrel due to geopolitical tensions. Adding to this, shipping and freight costs have risen dramatically, increasing pressure. Companies are absorbing these increases and focusing on exports, but this strategy faces questions about its long-term viability as global demand shifts. The sector's reliance on synthetic fibers, a key cost driver, exposes it to volatile commodity prices, raising concerns over future profitability.

item.title: US Fed Holds Rates Steady, Hints at Slower Cuts
item.description: The US Federal Reserve kept its benchmark interest rate steady at 3.5%-3.75% for the second meeting in a row. The FOMC vote, backed by Chair Jerome Powell and ten members, saw one dissenter favor a rate cut. The Fed restated its focus on fighting inflation and reaching maximum employment, acknowledging uncertainty in the economic outlook. While traders are raising bets on potential June rate cuts, leadership shifts at the Fed and global tensions add complexity.

item.title: RBI Injects ₹75,000 Crore to Manage Bank Liquidity
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is launching a three-day, ₹75,000 crore variable rate repo auction on March 20. This action addresses shrinking liquidity in the banking system, especially after recent tax payments. It shows the RBI's ongoing effort to manage short-term interest rates, even with an estimated surplus of over ₹81,963 crore.

item.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady, Citing Inflation Fears and Growth Worries
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady at 3.50%-3.75%, balancing persistent inflation with concerns over economic growth. Bitcoin prices dipped post-announcement, as investors await Fed Chair Jerome Powell's press conference for future policy direction.

item.title: Centrum Sells Housing Arm to Weaver With ₹1,450 Cr Funding
item.description: Centrum Group has completed the sale of its housing finance subsidiary to Weaver Services, backed by Premji Invest and Lightspeed Venture Partners. Weaver Services concurrently raised ₹1,450 crore to fund this acquisition, which aims to bolster its position in the affordable housing finance market. This strategic move by Centrum Group signifies a potential refocusing of its operations, while Weaver's substantial capital infusion highlights investor confidence in technology-driven financial platforms targeting underserved segments and expanding into Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.

item.title: CPCL's Top 'AAA' Credit Rating Holds Steady Amidst Major Refinery Project
item.description: Chennai Petroleum Corporation Limited (CPCL) has confirmed its credit ratings and bank facility details remain unchanged, according to Crisil Ratings. The company's total credit facilities stand at ₹16,000.90 crore, with its Cash Credit and Fund-Based Facilities rated 'AAA Stable' and Commercial Papers rated 'A1+'. This stability reflects CPCL's consistent financial standing, supported by its parent Indian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL).

item.title: India Corporate Tax Jumps 13%, But Other Taxes Lag Amid Global Risks
item.description: India's net direct tax revenue grew 7.1% to ₹22.8 lakh crore by March 17, boosted by a strong 13% rise in corporate tax collections. However, non-corporate advance tax payments fell 1.78%, pointing to uneven economic performance. Lower tax refunds also helped boost net collections. Meanwhile, escalating geopolitical tensions, like the Israel-Iran conflict, create significant economic uncertainty and threaten future revenue.

item.title: Iran Conflict Spurs Energy Security Shift, Fuels Inflation, Strains Banks
item.description: The Iran conflict has fundamentally shifted global energy priorities from cost efficiency to security, driving a rapid de-globalization of energy markets. This, along with supply chain disruptions affecting semiconductors and food, is fueling persistent inflation. Central banks face difficult policy choices, potentially capping asset returns and ushering in an era of heightened market volatility. Investors must prepare for sticky inflation and less accommodative policy, moving away from the post-2008 era of easy money.

item.title: Last Mile Enterprises Rolls Out Hydrogen Cooker for Indian Homes
item.description: Last Mile Enterprises is launching its innovative AquaFlame hydrogen cooking system, making fuel from water. This move supports India's National Green Hydrogen Mission. Pilot tests are scheduled, but exact commercial launch dates are still to be announced.

item.title: Religare Unit CHIL Fights ₹140 Cr Tax Demand, Eyes ₹96 Cr Reduction
item.description: Religare Enterprises' material subsidiary, Care Health Insurance (CHIL), has received income-tax demand orders totalling ₹140.20 Crores, including interest, for Assessment Years 2023-24 and 2024-25. CHIL disputes the tax computation, expecting a revised demand around ₹96 Crores post-rectification, and intends to file an appeal. The potential tax liability, if it stands, could affect the subsidiary's financial standing.

item.title: KIADB Orders Embassy Developments Unit to Surrender 78 Acres
item.description: Embassy Developments Ltd.'s subsidiary, Embassy East Business Park Limited, must surrender about 78 acres of land in Bengaluru by April 15, 2026. The Karnataka Industrial Areas Development Board (KIADB) issued the order, citing alleged breaches of a lease agreement. Embassy Developments denies the claims and intends to fight the order in court.

item.title: CreditAccess Grameen Raises $40 Million Via Secured Dollar Bonds
item.description: CreditAccess Grameen Limited has successfully completed the issuance of 4,000 secured, rated, and listed Non-Convertible Bonds worth USD 40 million. These dollar-denominated bonds carry a tenure of 60 months and are secured by a charge over the company's loan receivables and book debts. The move bolsters the company's debt capital base and diversifies its funding sources, while increasing its financial leverage.

item.title: Bharti Airtel Completes ₹15,696 Cr Rights Issue, Converts Shares
item.description: Bharti Airtel has converted 391 million partly paid shares after receiving ₹15,696 crore from the final call of its 2021 rights issue. This move increases the company's paid-up capital to ₹3,046.78 crore, making the converted shares fully equal to existing ones and ready for trading.

item.title: Anupam Rasayan Sees Credit Rating Reaffirmed After Jayhawk Acquisition Funding
item.description: CRISIL has reaffirmed Anupam Rasayan India Ltd.'s credit ratings at 'A+/Stable' and 'A1', lifting them from a 'Rating Watch with Negative Implications'. This positive action follows the company's successful $150 million funding for its U.S. acquisition of Jayhawk Fine Chemicals LLC and notable improvements in working capital management. The company's solid business standing and financial health are key strengths.

item.title: PIGL Buys ₹3.13 Cr Stake, Lifts Peaton Control to 60%
item.description: Power & Instrumentation (Gujarat) Limited (PIGL) increased its stake in subsidiary Peaton Electrical Company Limited by 8.94% for ₹3.13 crore, raising its total shareholding to 60%. The acquisition aims to enhance collaboration and PIGL's strategic influence.

item.title: India's 6G Race: Global Standards Dictate Spectrum, Raising Delay Fears
item.description: India is pushing for global leadership in 6G through initiatives like the Bharat 6G Alliance, aiming for a strong patent presence. However, its 6G spectrum allocation depends on international standards, creating a potential timeline risk. This echoes past 5G rollout delays and could impact India's competitiveness against countries like China and South Korea as commercial deployment targets 2030.

item.title: HDFC Bank Part-time Chair Resigns Citing Ethics; Interim Named
item.description: HDFC Bank's Part-time Chairman, Atanu Chakraborty, resigned on March 18, 2026, citing concerns over "happenings and practices" not aligning with his values. The RBI has approved Keki Mistry's appointment as interim Part-time Chairman for three months, effective March 19, 2026. This abrupt departure signals potential internal governance issues at India's largest private lender.

item.title: India's Advance Tax Collection Up 6% on Corporate Gains, Faces Geopolitical Risks
item.description: India's advance tax collections climbed 6% in FY26, driven by corporate contributions, even as non-corporate tax receipts dipped 2%. Analysts expect the government to meet its ₹24.2 lakh crore direct tax target, but remain cautious about potential impacts from the West Asia crisis and the lingering effects of personal income tax rate revisions.

item.title: Nippon India Small Cap Fund Delivers 20%+ CAGR, Turns ₹1 Lakh into ₹16 Lakh in 15 Years
item.description: Nippon India Small Cap Fund has delivered over 20% CAGR for both 10 and 15 years. An initial ₹1 lakh investment has grown to ₹16 lakh since its launch 15 years ago. Despite a 'Very High Risk' classification, the fund shows strong performance metrics and outpaces rivals and benchmarks through a diversified, growth-focused strategy in key sectors.

item.title: Weaver Services Acquires Centrum Housing Finance for ₹1,450 Crore
item.description: Weaver Services Pvt. Ltd. has completed its acquisition of Centrum Housing Finance Ltd. (CHFL), marking its formal entry into India's housing finance sector. The deal was supported by a ₹1,450 crore fundraising round led by Premji Invest and Lightspeed, with a strategy to build a scalable lending platform for India's underserved mortgage market and consolidate smaller housing finance firms.

item.title: KKR Invests $310M to Grow India's Electric Bus Market
item.description: Private equity firm KKR plans to invest up to $310 million in India's electric mobility sector, focusing on Allfleet, an electric bus operator, and its manufacturer, PMI Electro. This marks KKR's first climate transition investment in India, aiming to speed up the country's transport decarbonization through electric buses. The funding will help expand Allfleet's electric bus fleet and operations in Indian cities, supporting KKR's worldwide goal for sustainable infrastructure.

item.title: Fortis Healthcare Shareholders Back ESOP 2026 for Employees and Subsidiaries
item.description: Fortis Healthcare shareholders have given strong backing to the company's new Employee Stock Option Scheme (ESOP) 2026. The plan will also cover employees at subsidiary and associate firms. The overwhelming approval, with over 96% support for subsidiary options, aims to boost employee motivation and retention.

item.title: India Knitwear Firms Raise Prices 7% as Costs Climb
item.description: Tirupur's domestic knitwear industry, valued at Rs 30,000 crore, will increase prices by 7% on items like innerwear, nightwear, and t-shirts. The South India Hosiery Manufacturers Association blames a 15% rise in production costs, driven by raw material price hikes of 15-40%. This strains MSMEs and signals wider industry challenges.

item.title: Ceinsys Tech Raises ₹130 Crore Via Warrant Conversion, Promoter Stake Rises
item.description: Ceinsys Tech Limited has completed the allotment of 30,96,515 equity shares following the exercise and conversion of convertible warrants, raising ₹130.03 crore. The company's issued and paid-up capital has increased, with the equity share capital now standing at ₹20,93,76,610. Promoter and promoter group shareholding saw a marginal rise to 50.88% post-allotment.

item.title: Mideast Crisis Fuels Oil Surge, Pressures Rupee; Jio IPO Nears
item.description: Middle East conflict is sending oil prices to multi-year highs, driving the Indian Rupee to record lows. Production cuts are happening amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Amid global uncertainty, Reliance Jio Platforms is preparing its IPO filing, targeting a $100-120 billion valuation. Meanwhile, Urban Company shares surged on strong buying, and Indian equities broadly extended gains with cautious momentum.

item.title: India Tax Revenue Jumps 7.2% on Strong Corporate Tax Gains
item.description: India's net direct tax collections grew 7.2% to Rs 22.80 lakh crore as of March 17, driven by a 12.75% surge in corporate taxes. Personal income tax saw a 2.7% rise. A 5.86% drop in tax refunds was linked to improved procedural efficiency and automated checks.

item.title: Blue Coast Hotels Wins SAT Stay on ₹85 Crore SEBI Recovery
item.description: Blue Coast Hotels gained significant relief as the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) stayed SEBI's ₹85 crore recovery proceedings. Announced March 18, 2026, the order halts immediate action pending the company's appeal.

item.title: NCLT Rejects JBF Industries Revival Plan, Sends Case Back to CoC
item.description: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), Ahmedabad Bench, has rejected the resolution plan for JBF Industries Limited. The decision, pronounced on March 18, 2026, has referred the matter back to the Committee of Creditors (CoC) within the ongoing Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP). This setback adds significant uncertainty to the company's revival efforts.

item.title: Crompton Greaves Faces ₹4.21 Cr Tax Demand; Second Order for AY 2019-20
item.description: Crompton Greaves Consumer Electricals has received a ₹4.21 crore tax demand, including interest, for Assessment Year (AY) 2019-20. This is the second such order for the same assessment year. The company stated the demand will not materially affect its financials or operations, though it follows prior tax scrutiny.

item.title: GAIL Invests ₹13.54 Cr for 49% Stake in Leafiniti Bioenergy to Expand CBG
item.description: GAIL (India) Limited is set to acquire a 49% equity stake in Leafiniti Bioenergy Private Limited for ₹13.54 crore. The deal aims to boost GAIL's presence in the Compressed Bio Gas (CBG) sector, utilizing Leafiniti's operational plant and paving the way for new projects. Leafiniti Bioenergy had a turnover of ₹28.42 crore in FY 2024-25.

item.title: India Tax Growth Slows to 7.2%, May Miss Ambitious Budget Targets
item.description: India's direct tax collections for FY26 have reached ₹22.80 lakh crore as of March 17, a 7.2% increase year-on-year. While this shows steady progress, the growth pace is slower than expected, partly due to a significant 5.86% drop in tax refunds. Corporate tax collections remain strong, but non-corporate tax growth is lagging, raising questions about meeting the Union Budget's ambitious targets amid strong economic forecasts.

item.title: Euro Pratik Sales Board to Consider Interim Dividend; Q3 Net Profit ₹13.6 Cr
item.description: Euro Pratik Sales Limited's Board of Directors will meet on March 23, 2026, to consider declaring an interim dividend for FY25-26. The company also closed its trading window from March 18 to March 25, 2026, for designated personnel, a standard practice ahead of financial updates.

item.title: Oil Shock Fuels Inflation Fears, Complicates Fed Rate Path
item.description: US stocks opened lower as investors braced for the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision. While a hold is expected, escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have driven oil prices to critical highs and exacerbated inflation fears. The hotter-than-expected Producer Price Index (PPI) data further complicates the Fed's balancing act between controlling inflation and supporting economic growth, potentially delaying expected rate cuts.

item.title: India Launches Fund to Buffer Economy from Oil Price Shocks
item.description: India has launched an Economic Stabilisation Fund to protect its economy from volatile crude oil prices and geopolitical risks linked to West Asia. The measure aims to keep prices stable, control inflation, and give policymakers more flexibility, bolstering the nation's economic resilience.

item.title: Eternal Limited Hit by ₹19.26 Cr GST Orders, Plans Appeal
item.description: Eternal Limited announced on March 18, 2026, it received two GST adjudication orders totaling ₹19.26 crore. The demands cover tax, penalties, and interest for April 2019-March 2020 and April 2022-March 2023. The company is confident in its case and plans to appeal, expecting no financial impact.

item.title: Santosh Fine Fab Sees Another Company Secretary Exit, Starts New Search
item.description: Santosh Fine Fab Limited announced Mrs. Niti Nilesh Jain has resigned as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, effective March 18, 2026. The company accepted the resignation and is now searching for a replacement. This marks continued turnover in the key governance position.

item.title: KKR Invests $310M in India's EV Buses to Scale Fleet
item.description: KKR is investing up to $310 million in India's electric bus sector through PMI Electro and its platform Allfleet. This marks KKR's first climate transition investment in India, aiming to deploy over 5,000 e-buses. The venture faces market challenges like policy dependence, infrastructure issues, and competition. KKR's broader fossil fuel investments also draw scrutiny.
